title: CandleRuntime State-Vector handle abstraction (SSM/RWKV parity for KV cache surface)
goal: Define StateVectorHandle + StateVectorOps as the SSM/RWKV equivalent of KvCacheHandle: capture/restore/evict per-model SSM or RWKV state vectors. Returned from kv_cache() for SSM variants as the prefix-reuse-equivalent surface (per refinement INF-9 feature_parity_detail: 'prefix-reuse-equivalent for SSMs').
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/state_vector.rs with StateVectorHandle, StateVectorOps, SSMStateSnapshot serde impls; adapter.rs kv_cache() variant routing.
unblock_conditions: MT-063 KvCacheOps trait + MT-085/086/087 SSM/RWKV base merged.
implementation_notes: Per refinement INF-9 feature_parity_detail: SSMs need prefix-reuse-equivalent. The KvCacheOps trait surface in MT-063 is intentionally generic so SSMs can implement it with SSM-state semantics: prefix_commit captures the current SSM/RWKV state into a serialisable buffer + content_hash of the prefix tokens; prefix_restore writes the buffer back into the model's mutable state; prefix_evict + evict_all manage the in-memory LRU. SSMStateSnapshot enum: `Mamba2 { conv_states: Vec<Tensor>, ssm_states: Vec<Tensor> }`, `RwkvV5 { token_shift: Vec<Tensor>, ssm: Vec<Tensor> }`, `RwkvV6 { ... }`, `RwkvV7 { ... }` — serde Serialize as raw bytes (Tensor to_vec + dtype + shape) so snapshots persist to disk via cross-session state restore (MT-117). On restore: validate snapshot variant matches model variant; restore tensors; cross-session restore additionally validates ModelId artifact_sha256 matches the snapshot's recorded sha256 (rejects restoring into a different model). For Transformer variant: kv_cache() returns the candle-native KV-cache wrapper (MT-093 implements actual transformer KV ops); SSM/RWKV variants return StateVectorHandle. KvCacheStats for SSMs reports state_vector_bytes + entries (not token KV pairs). Tests: commit state on Mamba2, generate, restore, generate same continuation -> deterministic; tamper hash -> Err; restore across mismatched variants -> Err. UUID v7 for StateVectorId per HBR-INT-008.
risk_if_missed: SSM prefix-reuse-equivalent missing; INF-9 feature parity incomplete; cross-session state restore (MT-117) has no primitive to build on.
validator_focus: Variant-mismatch restore returns explicit Err; tamper detection; deterministic continuation after commit/restore on Mamba2.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Variant-mismatch restore rejected with explicit Err.
Cross-session state restore artifact_sha256 binding enforced.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: The StateVectorHandle / SSMStateSnapshot / SSMTensorSnapshot / StateVectorSnapshotRecord types are well-designed; serde round-trip works; snapshot_hash with domain-separated SHA256 covers all fields; variant-mismatch + artifact_sha256-mismatch + content_hash-tamper + snapshot-bytes-tamper are all rejected with explicit errors; UUID v7 enforced on StateVectorId. PERSIST-TO-DISK path via write_file_artifact + ArtifactStore is wired. HOWEVER — and this is the disqualifying defect — the StateVectorHandle is COMPLETELY DECOUPLED from the live SSM model state. (1) At load time (adapter.rs:204-208, 232-236, 260-264, 288-292), every SSM model is initialised with an EMPTY SSMStateSnapshot (empty_mamba2_snapshot() / empty_rwkv_*_snapshot() — Vec::new() for all tensor lists). (2) prefix_commit captures `self.current_snapshot` from InMemoryStateVectorOps — which started empty and is NEVER updated from the live CandleMamba2Model.state or RWKV State; there is no path that reads the model's mutable SSM state and writes it into current_snapshot. (3) prefix_restore writes a snapshot into `self.current_snapshot` (InMemoryStateVectorOps) but has NO PATH to inject that snapshot back into the CandleMamba2Model.state / RWKV State held inside the Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn TransformerModel>>>. The e2e smoke test (candle_e2e_smoke.rs:403-441) only passes because every generate call invokes `locked.reset_generation_state()` (generate.rs:123) which wipes the state to a fresh State::new — so two generate calls with the same prompt produce the same output regardless of whether prefix_commit/prefix_restore were called. The test ASSERTS determinism that is guaranteed by reset, not by state restore. Net: MT-088's substantive contract — 'commit state on Mamba2, generate, restore, generate same continuation' — is NOT proven; the substrate it builds for MT-117 cross-session restore restores empty snapshots into a sidecar struct that never touches the model. Contract validator_focus item 'deterministic continuation after commit/restore on Mamba2' is structurally unverifiable with the current wiring.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002 — persist path emits an artifact via write_file_artifact (good evidence of ArtifactStore wiring) but the restore path does not exercise the live model, so the wire test for SSM state restore is not satisfied.
